text.title: Burndown Diagnosis Cheatsheet Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Burndown Diagnosis Cheatsheet. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Off-track burndown has exactly 4 named root causes: scope creep, capacity loss, blocker stack, estimate drift. Diagnosis must pick one (or rank multiple).
rule.rationale: Naming forces evidence-based diagnosis; without naming, 'we're behind' loops indefinitely.

rule.statement: Each named cause requires ≥1 concrete evidence row: scope (added story IDs), capacity (PTO/sick days), blockers (blocker IDs with age), estimates (original vs revised).
rule.rationale: Evidence prevents motivated reasoning; without it, blame defaults to capacity (least scary).

rule.statement: Each diagnosed cause gets a remediation with owner + due date. 'Try harder' is not a remediation.
rule.rationale: Diagnosis without remediation is theatre; the cheatsheet's value is the loop close.

rule.statement: Run diagnostic only mid-sprint (day ≥3, ≥3 days remaining). End-of-sprint = retro instead.
rule.rationale: Mid-sprint diagnostic allows in-sprint correction; end-of-sprint is post-mortem.

rule.statement: Archive each diagnostic to a pattern log; quarterly review names the dominant cause type for the team.
rule.rationale: Recurring patterns are the real signal; one-off diagnostics miss it.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-burndown-diagnosis-cheatsheet.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
